When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The SPACE action caused changes. Let me compare the before and after frames to understand what changed. The animation changed cells in the bbox [24, 25, 63, 63], which is the area with the purple and white rectangles. Let me check what the new state looks like.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after SPACE action
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation

print("=== BEFORE ===")
for node in before['nodes']:
    if node['id'] >= 10:  # Focus on main area objects
        print(f"  id={node['id']} color={node['color']} pixels={node['pixels']} boundary={node['boundary']}")

print("\n=== AFTER ===")
for node in after['nodes']:
    if node['id'] >= 10:
        print(f"  id={node['id']} color={node['color']} pixels={node['pixels']} boundary={node['boundary']}")

# Also check the key region in ASCII
ascii_after = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\n=== Key region after SPACE ===")
for i in range(24, 44):
    print(f"{i:2d}: {ascii_after[i][24:40]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see what happened with SPACE:
- The white rectangle (originally rows 34-43, 10x10) was split into two parts:
  - Top half became purple (rows 34-38, 5x10)
  - Bottom half remains white (rows 39-43, 5x10)
- The bottom charcoal bar lost one pixel (64 -> 63)
So SPACE seems to convert the top half of the white block to purple. The pattern in the top-left panel shows white on top and purple on bottom. After SPACE, the block now has purple on top and white on bottom - which is the REVERSE of the pattern shown.
Wait, let me reconsider. The top-left panel shows:
- White rectangle (top)
- Purple rectangle (bottom)
The main area had:
- Purple rectangle with gray border (top)
- White rectangle (bottom)
After SPACE, the bottom block became:
- Purple (top half)
- White (bottom half)
Hmm, so SPACE seems to have changed the white block by converting its top half to purple. This seems like it's copying the color pattern from the top-left panel or from the purple block above.
Let me think about what the goal might be. Looking at the top-right panel, it shows two small squares: one white and one purple, with a white bar below. This might be a target state indicator.
Actually, looking more carefully at the image provided, I can see:
- Top-left: white block above purple block (this seems to be a reference/template)
- Top-right: small white square and small purple square with a bar (HUD?)
- Main area: purple block with gray border, and now a purple-on-top white-on-bottom block
The goal might be to make the blocks match the pattern in the top-left panel. Currently the main area has purple then white, but the reference shows white then purple. So maybe I need to reverse the order?
Let me try SPACE again to see if it toggles or continues the pattern.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try SPACE again to see the pattern
action(['SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
